definition: A yellow pigment produced when the liver breaks down old red blood cells. High levels can cause jaundice (yellowing of skin/eyes).
Blood sugar — your body's main energy source. High fasting levels may indicate diabetes or pre-diabetes.
A waste product filtered by your kidneys. Elevated levels suggest your kidneys may not be filtering properly.
The protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen throughout your body. Low levels indicate anemia.
A 3-month average of your blood sugar levels. Used to diagnose and monitor diabetes.
Alanine aminotransferase — a liver enzyme. Elevated levels indicate liver inflammation or damage.
Thyroid Stimulating Hormone — controls how much thyroid hormone your body makes. Abnormal levels indicate thyroid issues.
